Ad Text:
Our client is an international defence supplier, who design, manufacture and integrate communications solutions for Australian and global customers. They are looking to bolster their systems engineering capability supporting a number of projects across the Land and Air sectors.

Individuals with experience across the entire systems engineering life-cycle (Design and Review, V&V, Test & Integration, Requirements Management) will be highly suitable.


The Role:



Enforce design, system and specifications as required.
Establishing test plans & procedures and test analysis
Implementation, integration, verification, and validation
System definition/high level, preliminary and detailed design
Provide requirements analysis and management with the use of software management tools such as; DOORs and/or UML.



Experience Required:



3-10 years' experience as a Systems Engineer or more
Engineering Degree in either of the following disciplines: Electrical, Communications, Electronics, Mechatronics, Software and/or Hardware
Experience in systems architecture, design & development, and test & evaluation.
Australian Citizenship essential; current or Reinstatable clearance ideal
Defence industry experience desirable.


Please only apply if you hold Australian Citizenship, can obtain and maintain a Defence Security Clearance and US International Traffic in Arms Regulations clearance procedures.

If you think you match the above criteria, please apply in Word format only. PDF is also accepted.

Kinexus is the leading supplier for recruitment solutions to the Australian Defence Industry bringing intelligent connections and ideas together to better our sovereign nations capabilities in the field, at sea or in the air.

All applications should include your resume as a word document.

For more information, visit our website www.kinexus.com.au or contact Lewis Landau on 0422 572 745.

#LI-LL1 #SCR-lewis-landau

12-month contract

$1250 - $1350 per day plus super

Hybrid work arrangements

We are seeking a Principal AI Engineer, with experience in delivering production-grade AI systems and can lead from the front. You will set the technical direction for AI Engineering, shape delivery outcomes, and guide teams in building scalable, secure, and compliant AI platforms within a complex, enterprise environment.

This is not a data science role — it’s for an engineer who deeply understands AI frameworks, architecture, and Azure, and who can translate strategic AI vision into robust, operational solutions.

Your responsibilities

Lead the design, development, and deployment of enterprise-grade AI systems aligned with governance and assurance standards.

Drive modern AI practices including MLOps, testing, continuous integration, and lifecycle management for production AI.

Deliver Generative AI solutions such as RAG pipelines, vector search, embeddings, and agent-based workflows.

Engage, influence, and educate stakeholders, translating technical trade-offs into actionable decisions that align with business goals.

Mentor and technically lead engineers, uplifting team capability while maintaining strong engineering discipline and code quality.

Your skills

Demonstrated experience in software/data engineering with experience delivering enterprise-scale AI/ML solutions and proven leadership

Practical knowledge of Responsible AI, privacy, compliance, and experience productionising AI in complex environments

Deep expertise in Python and modern AI/ML frameworks, including Generative AI and agentic systems

Extensive experience with Azure and cloud-native architectures in enterprise or regulated environments

Strong MLOps/AIOps capabilities, including automated deployment, monitoring, and scaling

Solid background in data engineering and API integration for embedding AI into existing systems

Apply Now!

Job Title: LEAD OF DESIGN TECHNOLOGY & AI STRATEGY
Ad Text:
Job Description: Lead of Design Technology & AI Strategy

Location: Melbourne, VIC

Reporting to: Principal / Director

 

VIA Architects:

 VIA is a commercial architecture firm, which delivers solutions across aged care, retirement and integrated living, multi-residential projects and in the data centre/mission critical sector. Based in Melbourne, we work on projects across the country.

 With a team of 40 people, VIA has maintained a culture of collaboration and strong teamwork. In continuing our journey to remain efficient, productive and seek continuous improvement, we looking to take our next step into optimising technology through AI initiatives.

 

Role Purpose:

 The Lead of Design Technology & AI Strategy will oversee the ethical integration, governance, and operational rollout of Artificial Intelligence across the practice. Naturally this begins with optimising our existing technology tools and how we apply this to problem solving. You will transition the firm from manual, fragmented workflows to a "data-rich" environment, ensuring our 40-strong team maintains a competitive edge in design quality and project profitability.

 

Key Responsibilities

 1. AI Governance & Risk (The "Safety" Layer)

·         Establish and maintain the firm’s AI Implementation Protocol.

·         Act as the internal auditor for "Human-in-the-Loop" compliance, ensuring all AI-assisted technical specs (fire ratings, structural spans) are verified against the National Construction Code (NCC) and Australian Standards.

·         Manage data sovereignty, ensuring client project data is never used to train public models.




2. Workflow Architecture & Automation

·         Develop and maintain a Private Knowledge Base (RAG): A "Firm Brain" trained on our unique specification libraries, past project data, and preferred detail sets.

·         Automate high-frequency, low-value tasks: feasibility massing, unit-mix optimization, and initial daylight/wind analysis.

·         Explore integrating AI rendering and ideation tools directly into our Revit and Rhino pipelines.

·         Evaluate and manage the firm’s AI software stack, ensuring a high ROI on subscription costs

 

3. Capability & Culture

·         Conduct monthly "AI Sprints" and workshops to upskill staff from Junior Designers to Associates.

·         Identify "AI Champions" within each project sector (Aged Care, Retirement, Mission Critical and multi-residential) to lead localized implementation.

·         Create a rhythm into our ways of working, which embraces AI, creativity and efficiency.

 

Required Skills & Experience

·         Architectural Pedigree: You will come with strong architectural knowledge and may be either registered or bring experience in BIM and technician skills. The context is critical to this position. You must speak the language of "DA," "Construction Documentation," and "Project Yield."

·         Advanced BIM Literacy: Expert-level knowledge of Revit and other design and rendering tools is required. You should understand how data flows and can be manipulated in current processes, to establish how AI could be applied to improve the quality and efficiency it can provide.

·         Prompt Engineering & Scripting: In ideal world proficiency, existing experience and skills in advanced prompting and a working knowledge AI integration would be highly desirable. Though we will consider applicants that have demonstrated experience and driven technology improvements across architectural software.

·         Australian Regulatory Knowledge: Deep familiarity with Victorian planning schemes and the NCC.

·         Strategic Mindset: The ability to look at a 40-person P&L and identify where AI can reduce "burn" on repetitive tasks, improve and enhance processes and other outcomes for the business.




The Ideal Candidate

You are likely a Senior Architect or BIM Manager who has pivoted into AI. You aren’t interested in AI replacing architects; you are interested in AI replacing the boring parts of being an architect so the team can focus on design excellence. Equally it could be enhancing other aspects of a practise.

 

Why this role is different at our practice:

·         We don't "hallucinate": We focus on Grounded AI that respects Australian building regulatory obligations.

·         IP is King: You will be the custodian of our design and process legacy, using AI to amplify our "signature" and ways of working rather than diluting it.

·         You will be part of re-imagining how and what staff focus on into the future.




Why VIA

We have a grounded culture, working as tight teams to deliver solutions across multiple projects. Communication, adaptability and composure are critical skills we utilise every day to adjust to change and enhance our problem solving. We are aware we could do things better than we do now, so ongoing improvement is important to us. You will be part of this and enjoy working alongside and building relationships with people all on the same page.

Apply now if this role and environment is of interest, and you can bring a depth of critical skills in architectural technology, change, process improvement and genuine interest in living and breathing what AI might be able to do for a practise.

Category:
Architects (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
The role: 

Queensland Health is currently undertaking one of the largest health infrastructure programs ever seen in Australia. The largest of these, the Hospital Rescue Plan (HRP) is a visionary program designed to bring service and capabilities to more Queenslanders than ever before. The Hospital Rescue Plan will deliver 3 new hospitals, a new Queensland Cancer Centre, and the expansion of 11 existing hospitals resulting in 2,600 additional beds across Queensland.

The Solution Architects will act as the technical Subject Matter Expert (SME) across capacity expansion projects in one or more of the digital domains, work collaboratively with the broader capital expansion program team, HHS Digital teams, eHealth Queensland team and other key stakeholders.

This role will participate and guide the organisation in one or more of these technical specialties across the Hospital Rescue Plan:  

Core facility infrastructure - inform the design and commissioning of communications rooms, structured cabling, antenna systems, etc, to support the expansion.  
Wired and wireless communication networks - inform the design and commissioning of the digital network and the establishment of connectivity with external organisations and telecommunications providers. Gather and provide user and device information to enable the low-level configuration of the digital network and systems.  
Telephony and Messaging - inform the design and commissioning of telephony and messaging solutions to support the project including wired and wireless handset devices.  
End user computing (EUC) - inform the procurement/transition and installation of computers, printers and other end user devices for the project including profiling and software deployment / configuration.  
Servers and storage - inform the provisioning of server and storage capacity required to support the project.  
Audio Visual - inform the design and commissioning of audio-visual solutions required to support the project.  
Facility systems - inform the design and commissioning of facility systems including nurse call, patient entertainment, location services, check-in systems, digital wayfinding etc. and their integration with State-wide systems and the telephony and messaging platforms (MIE) required to support the project.  
Engineering systems - inform the design and commissioning of engineering systems including BMS, Access Control, Security, etc required to support the project.  
Major medical and biomedical systems - inform the design and commissioning of biomedical systems required to support the project.  

Are you right for this role?

Within the context of the key responsibilities described above, the ideal applicant will be someone who can demonstrate the following: 

Proven expertise in the development of Infrastructure architecture for complex systems inclusive of all architectural domains and ensuring consistency with specified requirements agreed with both internal and external stakeholders.  
Proven expertise in applying Infrastructure architecture principles, practices and processes within enterprise architecture, ICT investment and governance frameworks.  
Demonstrated analytical, problem solving and decision-making abilities to identify, analyse and resolve technology challenges in response to business and ICT requirements.  
Ability to establish and influence positive, strategic partnerships and collaborative working relationships with key senior executive stakeholders and key decision makers both internal and external to Queensland Health (SFIA: RLMT – Stakeholder Relationship Management Level 6). 

The following experience will be highly regarded:

Extensive knowledge in both healthcare and construction sectors
Professional and tertiary level qualifications in ICT, system engineering and solution architecture or similar

To be considered for this exciting opportunity submit your CV or resume and a short statement (maximum 2 pages) addressing your experience in each of the key areas outlined above.

Company: Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR)
Ad Text:
About the Opportunity 

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) is searching for a experienced, skilled and forward-thinking AI Engineer to help build and embed AI capability within a modern, intelligence-led regulatory environment. You will focus on delivering measured, trustworthy, and production-ready AI solutions that support real regulatory decisions and safety outcomes.

Reporting to the Senior Manager Data Management and Platform, you will design and implement AI and generative AI solutions aligned to ONRSR’s regulatory intelligence strategy, while contributing to the development of a reference architecture for AI, including generative and agentic systems, within a governed environment. You will build and operate end-to-end AI pipelines spanning data ingestion, retrieval, evaluation, deployment and monitoring, ensuring solutions are secure, compliant, explainable and aligned with data governance standards. Working closely with stakeholders, you will embed AI in ways that are trusted, practical and fit-for-purpose within a regulatory context.

In consultation with fellow subject matter experts, you will support the design and delivery of ONRSR’s Reference Architecture for generative and agentic infrastructure. Additionally, you will ensure our data pipelines meet our protection and governance guidelines while aligning to business needs, monitoring the consumption, usage and service levels of our solutions to ensure appropriate use and system performance. 

If you are passionate about impactful solution design, have a disciplined engineering background and you are looking to apply your strengths critical thinking, problem solving and relationship building skills, this may be the right opportunity for you. 

About you – your impact and contribution

The successful candidate will have demonstrated experience in designing, building and operating scalable AI pipelines across data collection, evaluation, deployment and monitoring, with further experience working on NLP, large language models or document understanding/analysis applications. 

You will be passionate about AI adoption, demonstrating active use of modern AI tools in the development process. As a key contributor in the Safety and Regulatory Insights team, you will have experience with cloud platforms and associated AI/ML services alongside your ability to develop high trust retrieval-based systems and agentic tools.

The ideal candidate will possess: 

Experience designing and deploying AI/ML solutions in production environments.
Strong experience with NLP, LLMs, and/or document understanding systems.
Experience building retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems and agentic workflows.
Deep understanding of model evaluation, prompt design, and non-deterministic system behaviour.
Experience working with cloud-based AI platforms (e.g. Microsoft Fabric, Azure AI, or similar ecosystems).
Ability to integrate AI solutions with data platforms, pipelines and governance frameworks.
Strong software engineering practices, including versioning, testing and monitoring of AI systems.
Experience working in regulated or high-trust environments.
Understanding of AI risk, bias, explainability and safe deployment practices.
Ability to define and communicate limitations and appropriate use of AI systems.
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences. 
Experience working in Agile delivery environments.
A proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and practical outcomes.
Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively connect and communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders.  
Experience with Agile methodology, with a history of leading teams in Agile processes.  

Our Organisation and Culture

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) has the responsibility for regulatory oversight of rail safety throughout Australia. As an independent regulatory authority, ONRSR pursues safe railways for Australia by encouraging safe rail operations, enforcing national compliance with the Rail Safety National Law and promoting and improving rail safety across the country.

Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong commitment to its Culture Blueprint - the foundation for our purposeful, diverse and inclusive workplace and a key contributor to how we achieve our vision of Safe Railways for all Australians.

We are passionate about supporting Our People

At ONRSR we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for Rail Safety for all Australians. Our colleagues have an impact far beyond work, and you’ll be part of a team that is contributing to enhancing safety outcomes.

You will be part of a $26 billion industry employing over 142,000 people that is at the forefront of innovation, productivity, and world’s best practice.
We invest in building the capability of our people through customised learning and development programs and by growing your curiosity and experience.
Employee Assistance Program and Peer Support Network.
Mentoring for your own development and the opportunity to mentor others.
Extra 3 days leave with Christmas closedown for all colleagues.
Salary packaging of car.

We support healthy work life balance, encourage ongoing learning and growth, and offer flexible work options to help our people succeed both professionally and personally. Whether you’re building new skills or deepening your expertise, you’ll find a team environment that’s focused on connection, contribution, and continuous improvement. Further details on the benefits of working at ONRSR can be found at https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

The application process

Not sure you meet all the requirements? That’s okay — we’re interested in people who may not tick all the boxes. If this sounds like a place you’d thrive, we’d love to hear from you. 

To Apply, please submit your resume along with a cover letter outlining why you are interested in the role and how your experience aligns with what we are looking for.

More information can be found in the Position Description which is attached below and can also be found at: https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

As part of the recruitment and selection process, shortlisted candidates will be required to undertake a national criminal history check. Please note that the outcome of this check will be considered in context and does not automatically disqualify applicants from progressing.

Enquiries to: ONRSR’s People and Capability team by emailing?recruitment@onrsr.com.au? 

Salary: $142,416 - $168,216 + 12% super

Applications close: Wednesday, 22 April 2026, 6:00PM

At ONRSR, we’re committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, including those of diverse gender identities, sexual orientations, cultures, religions, ages, abilities and neurodiversity.

If there’s anything we can do to make the recruitment process more accessible or comfortable for you, please let us know – we’re here to support you.

We also commit to responding to every applicant.

NOTE:

You must be an Australian citizen, permanent resident or hold a valid work permit or visa, as we are unable to provide sponsorship.
Applications are sought from direct candidates only. No agencies please.
Application closing dates are at the discretion of ONRSR and may change without notice.

Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively connect and communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders.  
Experience with Agile methodology, with a history of leading teams in Agile processes.  

Our Organisation and Culture

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) has the responsibility for regulatory oversight of rail safety throughout Australia. As an independent regulatory authority, ONRSR pursues safe railways for Australia by encouraging safe rail operations, enforcing national compliance with the Rail Safety National Law and promoting and improving rail safety across the country.

Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong commitment to its Culture Blueprint - the foundation for our purposeful, diverse and inclusive workplace and a key contributor to how we achieve our vision of Safe Railways for all Australians.

We are passionate about supporting Our People

At ONRSR we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for Rail Safety for all Australians. Our colleagues have an impact far beyond work, and you’ll be part of a team that is contributing to enhancing safety outcomes.

You will be part of a $26 billion industry employing over 142,000 people that is at the forefront of innovation, productivity, and world’s best practice.
We invest in building the capability of our people through customised learning and development programs and by growing your curiosity and experience.
Employee Assistance Program and Peer Support Network.
Mentoring for your own development and the opportunity to mentor others.
Extra 3 days leave with Christmas closedown for all colleagues.
Salary packaging of car.

We support healthy work life balance, encourage ongoing learning and growth, and offer flexible work options to help our people succeed both professionally and personally. Whether you’re building new skills or deepening your expertise, you’ll find a team environment that’s focused on connection, contribution, and continuous improvement. Further details on the benefits of working at ONRSR can be found at https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

The application process

Not sure you meet all the requirements? That’s okay — we’re interested in people who may not tick all the boxes. If this sounds like a place you’d thrive, we’d love to hear from you. 

To Apply, please submit your resume along with a cover letter outlining why you are interested in the role and how your experience aligns with what we are looking for.

More information can be found in the Position Description which is attached below and can also be found at: https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

As part of the recruitment and selection process, shortlisted candidates will be required to undertake a national criminal history check. Please note that the outcome of this check will be considered in context and does not automatically disqualify applicants from progressing.

Enquiries to: ONRSR’s People and Capability team by emailing recruitment@onrsr.com.au  

Salary: $142,416 - $168,216 + 12% super

Applications close: Wednesday, 22 April 2026, 6:00PM

At ONRSR, we’re committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, including those of diverse gender identities, sexual orientations, cultures, religions, ages, abilities and neurodiversity.

If there’s anything we can do to make the recruitment process more accessible or comfortable for you, please let us know – we’re here to support you.

We also commit to responding to every applicant.

NOTE:

You must be an Australian citizen, permanent resident or hold a valid work permit or visa, as we are unable to provide sponsorship.
Applications are sought from direct candidates only. No agencies please.

Position Description - AI Engineer

Company description:



Accelerate AI adoption. Influence how we hire.

Established in 1840, we are Australia's first wellbeing company, delivering health, wealth and care services for our members and the community.

As a social enterprise, we're motivated by our purpose to positively impact the wellbeing of millions. We reinvest profits to deliver products and services sustainably that matter most to our members, customers and the Australian community.

For us, Real Wellbeing encompasses the whole experience of life. It's about feeling supported in your health, connected with your community and empowered to live life on your terms.



Job description:



Our Opportunity:

You will lead the end‑to‑end adoption of AI solutions within our People & Culture function - from identifying opportunities to delivering real business value. A major early focus will be partnering with our recruitment platform vendor to ensure their AI models meet our standards for fairness, transparency, performance, and compliance.

In this role, you will:

Lead end‑to‑end AI adoption within a business squad - from identifying opportunities and shaping requirements to solution selection, delivery, and post‑launch support.
Work directly with AI vendors - challenging assumptions, asking the right technical questions, evaluating model behaviour, ensuring fairness and performance, and influencing product direction.
Partner with internal technical and business stakeholders to shape use cases, drive adoption, build user confidence, and ensure AI solutions deliver measurable business value.
Ensure all AI initiatives meet governance, risk, and compliance expectations, and that solutions are well‑designed, documented, and aligned with enterprise standards.
Contribute to the evolution of enterprise‑level AI practices in an area such as AI literacy & change, AI governance, or AIOps / AI innovation.

You'll be part of a small, expert group helping Australian Unity become an AI‑native enterprise - and you'll have the freedom to shape how we get there.

About You:

We're looking for someone with a strong technical foundation who is ready to apply it in a more product‑oriented role. You may come from a software engineering or computer science background with hands‑on AI experience, and you've either already made the move into product-style work - or you're excited to take that next step.

You enjoy solving business problems, engaging with stakeholders, and challenging vendors with the right technical questions. You're as comfortable discussing model behaviour and risks as you are explaining concepts to non‑technical audiences.

To be successful in the role you will be able to demonstrate the following capabilities / experience:

Hands‑on industry experience (3+ years) in software engineering, AI engineering, data science, or computer science
Practical experience applying AI/ML/LLM technologies in real-world projects
Experience translating business needs into technical or product requirements
Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify complex AI concepts for non‑technical audiences
Experience delivering solutions in regulated or risk-aware environments (privacy, fairness, model behaviour) is a plus
Exposure to AI governance, AI literacy/change, AIOps/MLOps/LLMOps, model evaluation, or data privacy considerations (desirable)
Hands-on experience with low-code and automation platforms e.g., Microsoft Power Automate, Copilot Studio (desirable)

What's on Offer:

You will enjoy a range of great employee benefits and rewards including:

Competitive salary + bonus program
Enjoy additional yearly Well-Being and Community leave days
14-week paid parental leave, with equal benefit for both parents
Employee Referral Program
Employee Assistance Program - support and assistance for you and your partner at those times in life when you need it most
Maxxia Rewards - a great range of discounts and benefits at selected retail outlets, department stores, attractions, travel, cinemas, restaurants.
Discounts across the Australian Unity business including Private Health Insurance, General Insurance + more!
Available access to LinkedIn Learning courses through our great Learning platform

For further benefits visit: https://www.australianunity.com.au/careers/culture

Australian Unity is an Equal Opportunity employer and we encourage applications from all members of the community, including people of Aboriginal and Torres strait Islander descent, culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds and, mature aged people.

Location: Berkeley Vale, Gosford & Central Coast NSW
Ad Text:
Data and AI Engineer

(Agentic AI, Fabric & Manufacturing Integrations) 📍 Berkeley Vale, NSW | Hybrid

At TrendPac, we’re a fast‑growing FMCG manufacturer with a strong focus on operational excellence, automation and data‑driven decision making. As we continue to invest heavily in modern data platforms and applied AI, we’re looking for a Data and AI Engineer to help design, build and scale enterprise‑grade data and AI solutions that deliver real, measurable value across the business.

This is a hands‑on role suited to someone who enjoys working close to the business, translating complex operational problems into robust data products and AI‑enabled solutions.

About the role

As our Data and AI Engineer, you will work closely with senior leaders and operational teams across Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Quality, Finance and IT. You’ll play a key role in shaping and executing TrendPac’s Data and AI strategy, building modern data platforms and applied AI solutions that support smarter decision‑making, automation and performance uplift across our manufacturing environment.

You’ll bring strong expertise across Microsoft Fabric, Azure and modern AI/agentic architectures, with the opportunity to work on real‑world manufacturing integrations including MES and SCADA systems.

Key responsibilities
Assist in defining and executing TrendPac’s Data and AI vision, strategy and roadmap
Design, build and optimise end‑to‑end data products and AI solutions aligned to business needs
Architect and develop Microsoft Fabric solutions including lakehouse design, medallion architecture, semantic models and governed data layers
Build and maintain ETL / ELT pipelines, orchestration workflows and dimensional models to support analytics and AI use cases
Design and implement enterprise‑grade RAG solutions using Azure OpenAI and modern LLM frameworks
Develop agentic AI workflows and MCP‑style integrations to enable reliable, auditable AI automation
Build AI‑enabled workflows using Copilot Studio, Power Apps, Power Automate and AI Builder, including OCR‑based extraction
Deliver application components where required using Python (FastAPI), React (TypeScript) and PostgreSQL
Integrate with manufacturing systems such as MES and SCADA, enabling operational KPIs and OEE‑style metrics
Partner with stakeholders to define clear data contracts, metrics and semantic definitions
Ensure solutions are secure, scalable and compliant with governance and security standards
Support solution quality through testing, documentation, troubleshooting and continuous improvement
Act as a subject matter expert, uplifting data and AI capability across the organisation

About you

You’re a technically strong engineer who enjoys solving practical business problems. You’re comfortable working end‑to‑end — from architecture and development through to deployment and stakeholder engagement — and you thrive in environments where your work has visible impact.

You will bring:

Strong experience with Microsoft Fabric and modern data engineering patterns
Hands‑on experience with Azure OpenAI, agentic LLM architectures and RAG implementations
Advanced skills in Python and SQL, with solid Azure cloud experience
Experience designing and delivering ETL / ELT pipelines and analytical data models
Exposure to manufacturing or FMCG environments (highly regarded, not essential)
Experience integrating data from operational systems such as MES, SCADA or ERP (well regarded)
Experience delivering AI‑enabled business workflows and automation
Strong communication skills and the ability to work effectively with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

Essential criteria
Microsoft Azure Data Engineer Associate or Fabric Data Engineer Associate certification
Bachelor’s degree in IT, Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science or a related discipline

Why join TrendPac?
Work on real, applied AI and data solutions in a live manufacturing environment
Be part of a business that genuinely invests in modern platforms, automation and capability uplift
High visibility role with exposure to senior leaders and operational teams
Flexible working arrangements and a supportive, down‑to‑earth culture
Opportunity to shape how AI and data are embedded across the organisation

About the Role

RPMGlobal (RPM) is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Data Scientist to join our team working on advanced analytics and data-driven products within a modern Azure-based platform.

This is a permanent full-time role based in our Brisbane CBD headquarters. This position is in-office; however, it allows for ad hoc working from home. 

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working across both data science and data engineering disciplines — designing scalable data pipelines, optimising SQL-based systems, and building intelligent solutions leveraging large language models.




Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and optimise ETL pipelines using Microsoft SQL Server

Implement and maintain incremental data processing using Change Tracking / CDC

Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps

Develop and deploy data science solutions, including LLM-based features

Work with Azure cloud services to build scalable, production-grade solutions

Collaborate with product and engineering teams to deliver data-driven features

Troubleshoot and performance tune complex SQL workloads




About you

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field

Strong expertise in Microsoft SQL Server, including:

ETL development

Query performance tuning

Change Data Capture (CDC) and Change Tracking

Experience with Azure DevOps, including CI/CD pipeline implementation

Hands-on experience with large language models and tools such as Semantic Kernel

Strong experience working with the Azure cloud platform

Proven track record in developing commercial engineering or technical applications

Strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset

Strong command of the English language in both verbal and written form

Must have the legal right to work in Australia




Highly Regarded Experience

Experience in data engineering or building data platforms

Experience with Databricks, Azure OneLake, or similar lakehouse technologies

Exposure to distributed data processing frameworks (e.g. Spark)

Experience in the mining or resources industry




About RPM

RPMGlobal is the mining industry’s leading enterprise technology and training provider and has been at the forefront of change and innovation in the mining industry for over 50 years.​​

We advance the mining industry towards a safer, more efficient, and more sustainable future through the delivery of innovative software solutions and deep domain mining expertise. Guided by our underlying principles of mining expertise, innovation, and sustainability, we strive to create safer and more efficient operations for our mining customers.

Our focus on providing advanced mining enterprise technology and training across the mining lifecycle enables us to guide customers through the industry's current and emerging challenges worldwide. This includes helping them meet the shift in social norms and consumer and investor ESG-related expectations. 




Please, no agencies or recruiters.

Do you enjoy designing, building and maintaining modern data platforms? Are you keen to further develop your data engineering skills while contributing to a growing Data & Analytics capability? We’re looking for a Data Engineer to join us in this newly created role.

As a Data Engineer, you will collaborate with state, regional and national teams, as well as ICT, to enhance data product consumption, data quality and reliability. You will play a key role in developing and operating data pipelines, datasets and data products, supporting both operational and head office reporting through our Data & Analytics platform.

 

Key Responsibilities

Contribute to the implementation and day‑to‑day operation of the Data Platform in Microsoft Fabric.
Design, build, test and maintain reliable, scalable data pipelines to support ingestion and transformation of data in to data products.
Integrate data from multiple sources and technologies to support a unified, scalable data ecosystem where data is accurate, reliable and accessible.
Assist with the design and optimisation of data models and data products to support performance, integrity and security.
Implement data quality processes including validation, cleansing and regular checks to help maintain trusted data.
Work with ICT and business teams to understand requirements and deliver practical, efficient data solutions that support reporting and analytics.
Maintain clear documentation for data pipelines, data processes and solutions to support supportability and continuous improvement.


What You’ll Need (Data Engineer)

Bachelor’s degree in information technology, Computer Science or a related field.
Hands‑on experience supporting and developing data platforms e.g., Microsoft Fabric or Databricks.
Working knowledge of modern lakehouse concepts and core technologies such as Lakehouse / OneLake / Data Lake, Spark, Data Factory and SQL Analytics (or equivalents).
Experience building and maintaining scalable data pipelines using ETL/ELT patterns.
Proficiency in one or more of Python, PySpark, T‑SQL
Experience with engineering practices such as Git/GitHub, CI/CD, and working with notebooks.
Solid grounding in SQL and relational database concepts (e.g., MS SQL).
Exposure to agile ways of working and DataOps/DevOps practices.
Familiarity with Power BI is beneficial.


Who we are

REMONDIS is one of the world's largest recycling, service and water companies. We're a privately owned family company headquartered in Germany, with over 40,000 employees and operations on four continents. We've been here in Australia since 1982.


Are you with us? To Apply: 

You'll need a copy of a proof of full working entitlement in Australia.
You'll undergo a police check and pre-employment medical, including a drug and alcohol test.

REMONDIS is an Equal Opportunity Employer and we invite you to be part of an organisation that fosters a diverse workplace. Aboriginal
and Torres Strait Islander people are strongly encouraged to apply.
